evidence_refs.quote: Tool-augmented language model agents suspend generation during external tool calls and must reconstruct post-pause state upon resumption, typically by re-invoking the full teacher model on the entire context. claims.claim: Our contribution is threefold: (1) we establish that the distillation target is learnable under controlled conditions, (2) we show through ablation that both pre-pause intent and tool results are individually necessary and jointly substantially more informative, and (3) we quantify the compaction ratio and inference cost of the compact representation.

claims.claim: Let a tool-pause trace consist of: - A **pre-pause intent** vector $x_{\text{ctx}}$ summarizing the agent's state before the tool call, - A **post-pause tool result** vector $x_{\text{tool}}$ encoding the tool output, - A **resume action** $y$ drawn from a finite action set, determined by a teacher/oracle policy $\pi^*(x_{\text{ctx}}, x_{\text{tool}})$.
